claims.evidence_text: The critical level for South Dakota wheat is 16 ppm (Olsen method). Research conducted at 33 sites in South Dakota shows that P fertilizer application increased yield by 36% in the very low soil test P level (0-3 ppm) soils.
claims.claim_summary: Critical soil-test P level for wheat is 16 ppm (Olsen); P fertilization raised yield 36% on very low (0-3 ppm) soils.

claims.evidence_text: The critical level for South Dakota wheat is 160 ppm K. Potassium fertilizer recommendations for wheat are based on the soil K test and the yield goal.
claims.claim_summary: Critical soil-test K level for wheat is 160 ppm (ammonium acetate method).

claims.evidence_text: Dry sulfur fertilizer materials can be applied with the seed at rates of 10-15 lb S/a. Elemental sulfur should be applied well before wheat is planted because the material requires 1 to 3 months in warm soil before it is completely available.
claims.claim_summary: Dry sulfur fertilizer can be applied with wheat seed at 10-15 lb S/acre; rate based on soil test, texture and tillage.
